title: Helios — N-Body Studio
description: A real-time gravitational N-body studio: Barnes-Hut O(n log n) solver, SEVEN integrators including 4th- AND 6th-order Yoshida symplectic schemes (Yoshida 6 holds energy to ~1e-13, ~40,000× flatter than Yoshida 4 — near machine precision). NEWEST — THE RESONANCE ATLAS: Laskar's FREQUENCY-MAP ANALYSIS swept across a whole 2-D family of orbits, the technique behind the diffusion portrait of the Solar System and the asteroid belt's resonance web (the 'Arnold web'). Every pixel launches a planar-circular-restricted-three-body test particle from its own osculating ellipse (a,e), integrates it (RK4, conserving the Jacobi constant to ~1e-9), and records the inertial signal Z(t)=(x+iy)·e^{i·t} whose dominant NAFF frequency is the orbit's mean motion n (the Keplerian n=a^(−3/2) recovered to ~1e-6); measuring n on the first vs the second half of the record gives the frequency diffusion log|Δn/n| — frozen (≲−6) on an invariant torus, large (≳−2) in the chaotic resonance-overlap zones. Coloured by frequency the live, progressively-filled heatmap shows the resonance plateaus, by diffusion the bright chaotic threads of the mean-motion resonances n/n_J=p/q (the Chirikov-overlap route to the Kirkwood gaps); click any cell to drill into that orbit's TIME-FREQUENCY SPECTROGRAM (a sliding-window Hann FFT + a NAFF fundamental ridge) — dead-straight for a regular orbit, a wandering, smeared ridge for a chaotic one: frequency diffusion made visible. NEW — GENERAL RELATIVITY: a first post-Newtonian (1PN) correction about the dominant mass, a₁ₚₙ = (μ/c²r³)[(4μ/r−v²)r + 4(r·v)v], wired into every integrator with a momentum-conserving reaction on the central body, so eccentric orbits precess into rosettes — the mechanism behind Mercury's 43″/century perihelion advance. A RELATIVITY LAB integrates a body on a 4th-order Runge-Kutta, detects its periapsis passages and averages the azimuthal advance, then checks the measured precession head-to-head against the closed-form Δϖ = 6πμ/(c²a(1−e²)) per orbit (exact in the weak field, departing by the genuine higher-order PN terms as v/c grows) — and feeds the same formula Mercury's real numbers to recover 42.98″/century. NEW — GRAVITATIONAL WAVES: a WAVE LAB integrates the relative orbit with the 2.5PN radiation reaction a_RR = (8/5)(G²Mμ/c⁵r³)[(3v²+17/3·GM/r)ṙ n̂ − (v²+3GM/r)v], so the binary inspirals; it draws Einstein's quadrupole-formula transverse-traceless strain h₊/h× (the LIGO 'chirp', frequency = 2× orbital), sonifies it into the audible band, watches an eccentric orbit circularise, and checks the integrated merger time head-to-head against Peters' (1964) t_c = 5c⁵a⁴/256G³m₁m₂M (eccentricity-aware) — the reaction force and the closed form derived independently, agreeing to <1%. NEWEST — STRONG-FIELD GRAVITY: a BLACK HOLE LAB that leaves the post-Newtonian regime behind and integrates the EXACT null geodesics of the Schwarzschild metric, u'' = −u + 3M u² (the lone 3Mu² term IS general relativity). A from-scratch reverse RAY TRACER shoots a photon backward per pixel and integrates its geodesic until it crosses the horizon (the black SHADOW) or escapes to sample a procedural sky — so the background is gravitationally LENSED into an Einstein ring — gathering a thin ACCRETION DISC on circular geodesics with the exact relativistic frequency shift g = √(1−3M/r)/(1−Ωℓ) beaming the approaching side (I ∝ g⁴) and the disc's far side lensed up over the top: the Event-Horizon-Telescope / Interstellar image, rendered progressively row by row on a Canvas. From the same orbit equation fall the photon sphere r = 3M, the ISCO r = 6M, light deflection (Einstein's 4M/b far out, diverging logarithmically — matching Bozza 2002 — as the impact parameter nears the critical b_c = 3√3 M that sets the shadow size), and the strong-field precession 2π(1/√(1−6M/r)−1) per orbit (diverging at the ISCO, reducing to the 1PN 6πM/r far out). Plus the exact analytic KERR shadow rim — the famous D-shape traced by the unstable spherical photon orbits (Bardeen/Teo), flattened and displaced by frame dragging, collapsing to the 3√3 M circle as the spin vanishes. NEWER STILL — SYMPLECTIC PLANETARY DYNAMICS: a SYMPLECTIC LAB built on a from-scratch WISDOM–HOLMAN integrator (democratic-heliocentric splitting, Duncan-Levison-Lee 1998) whose Kepler drift advances each planet along its osculating ellipse EXACTLY via a universal-variable propagator (Stumpff functions + a bracketed Newton solve for the universal anomaly, good for any eccentricity and either time direction, the Lagrange f·ġ−ḟ·g=1 identity verified to 1e-13) — so only the faint planet-planet perturbation is integrated numerically. It races WH (2nd order, and a 4th-order Yoshida triple-jump of it) head-to-head against velocity Verlet and RK4 on the identical unsoftened Hamiltonian at one coarse Δt: WH holds |ΔE/E| bounded ~10000× flatter than Verlet while the non-symplectic RK4 drifts secularly away — the reason long-term Solar-System integrations (SWIFT, MERCURY, REBOUND) all use WH — shown live on a log-scale energy-error plot and a top-down orbit view. A from-scratch CHAOS LAB integrates the variational (tangent) equations with the exact analytic tidal tensor to compute the maximal Lyapunov exponent (Benettin renormalisation) and the MEGNO indicator ⟨Y⟩ (Cincotta & Simó) — telling a regular orbit (⟨Y⟩→2) from a chaotic one (⟨Y⟩ grows linearly) live, with order/chaos classification and MEGNO/λ-vs-time plots. A SPECTRAL LAB implements Laskar's NAFF (Numerical Analysis of Fundamental Frequencies) on a hand-written FFT — a Hann-windowed correlation refined between FFT bins by golden-section search (super-resolution to ~1e-8 of a bin) — to read an orbit's fundamental frequency and harmonic line spectrum and a frequency-map diffusion chaos index (Laskar 1990), and a POINCARÉ LAB stamps a test particle's surface-of-section in the co-rotating frame of the two heaviest bodies, turning an invariant torus into a closed curve and chaos into a scattered cloud. Plus an osculating-orbit overlay that reconstructs the live Kepler ellipse of any body from its eccentricity vector, a restricted-three-body layer drawing the five Lagrange points and the zero-velocity (Hill-region) curves of the Jacobi integral, inelastic collisions & accretion, a shadow-copy orbit forecaster, live energy/momentum/virial diagnostics, sixteen astrophysical presets (a GR-precession rosette, the figure-eight choreography and its chaotic 'broken eight' twin, the Pythagorean three-body problem, Saturn's rings, Jupiter's Trojans, an eccentric Kepler showcase, co-orbital horseshoes & tadpoles, a hierarchical triple), colour-by speed/mass/acceleration, shareable permalinks, and an in-app 57-check numerical self-test that re-derives every physical claim live — including the frequency-map Atlas recovering the Kepler frequency law n=a^(−3/2) end-to-end, its RK4 conserving the Jacobi constant, its diffusion index separating a regular orbit from a chaotic one by ~5 decades, the spectrogram ridge staying flat for a tone yet tracking a chirp, the universal-variable Kepler propagator matching the analytic E−e·sinE solution to machine precision and Wisdom–Holman beating Verlet on energy by ~10000× while staying time-reversible — down to a finite-difference proof of the tidal tensor, the time-reversibility of the integrator, FFT round-trips, NAFF sub-bin super-resolution, Jacobi-constant conservation along a Poincaré section, the relativistic precession matching the closed-form GR formula, the gravitational-wave inspiral reproducing Peters' merger time, the quadrupole (1+cos²ι) polarisation pattern and energy balance dE/dt = −L, and the strong-field black-hole claims (the shadow's b_c = 3√3 M cross-checked against a bisection of the ray tracer, the 3M photon sphere and 6M ISCO, the 4M/b and Bozza-log light deflection, the exact precession formula, the √½ disc redshift at the ISCO and the Kerr shadow collapsing to a circle as spin → 0) — all hand-written TypeScript on a Canvas.
